scans.findings: Left Ventricle: Normal LV cavity dimensions. LVEF: 60%. Left Atrium: Normal (34 mm). Aortic Valve: Calcified bicuspid aortic valve with severe aortic stenosis. Peak velocity: 4.3 m/s, Mean gradient: 44 mmHg, Aortic valve area (AVA): 0.8 cm². Mitral Valve: Mild aortic regurgitation noted. Right Ventricle: Normal size. PASP: 35 mmHg.
scans.conclusion: Severe Calcified Aortic Stenosis (AVA 0.8 cm², Mean Gradient 44 mmHg) on Bicuspid Aortic Valve with Preserved LV Ejection Fraction (60%).

scans.findings: Left Ventricle: Normal LV cavity dimensions. LVEF: 60%. Left Atrium: Normal (34 mm). Aortic Valve: Calcified bicuspid aortic valve with severe aortic stenosis. Peak velocity: 4.3 m/s, Mean gradient: 44 mmHg, Aortic valve area (AVA): 0.8 cm². Mitral Valve: Mild aortic regurgitation noted. Right Ventricle: Normal size. PASP: 35 mmHg.
scans.conclusion: Severe Calcified Aortic Stenosis (AVA 0.8 cm², Mean Gradient 44 mmHg) on Bicuspid Aortic Valve with Preserved LV Ejection Fraction (60%).

scans.findings: Left Ventricle: LVEF: 38% (moderately reduced). Hypokinesia noted in anterior and anteroseptal wall segments. Left Atrium: Dilated (LA volume index 42 ml/m²). Mitral Valve: Secondary functional mitral regurgitation (Moderate MR). Right Ventricle: TAPSE 15 mm (borderline RV systolic function). Pericardium: Trace pericardial effusion.
scans.conclusion: Ischemic Cardiomyopathy with Moderately Reduced LVEF (38%), Anterior Wall Hypokinesia, and Moderate Functional Mitral Regurgitation.

scans.findings: Left Ventricle: Concentric LV hypertrophy present. LVEF: 62%. Regional wall motion normal. Left Atrium: Mildly dilated (LA dimension 38 mm). Valves: Aortic valve trileaflet with mild sclerosis, no stenosis. Mitral valve shows mild posterior leaflet prolapse with mild regurgitation (MR). Right Ventricle & Atrium: Normal dimensions. PASP: 28 mmHg. Pericardium: No pericardial effusion.
scans.conclusion: Concentric LV Hypertrophy with preserved LV Systolic Function (LVEF 62%), Grade I Diastolic Dysfunction, and Mild Mitral Regurgitation.
scans.indication: Hypertensive Heart Disease / Mild MR evaluation

scans.findings: Left Ventricle: LVEF: 55%. Normal global systolic function. Grade II Diastolic Dysfunction (pseudonormal pattern, E/A 1.4, Dec time 150 ms, E/e' 15). Left Atrium: Dilated (LA volume index 39 ml/m²). Valves: Mild Tricuspid Regurgitation. PASP: 38 mmHg (mild pulmonary hypertension). Pericardium: Intact.
scans.conclusion: Grade II LV Diastolic Dysfunction with Left Atrial Enlargement and Mild Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.

scans.findings: Left Ventricle: Normal LV size and wall thickness. LVEF: 65% (normal). No regional wall motion abnormality. Left Atrium: Normal size (32 mm). scans.conclusion: Normal Transthoracic Echocardiogram. Preserved Biventricular Systolic Function (LVEF 65%). No Valvular Heart Disease.

clinical_reports.findings: Left Ventricle: Normal LV cavity dimensions. LVEF: 60%. Left Atrium: Normal (34 mm). Aortic Valve: Calcified bicuspid aortic valve with severe aortic stenosis. Peak velocity: 4.3 m/s, Mean gradient: 44 mmHg, Aortic valve area (AVA): 0.8 cm². Mitral Valve: Mild aortic regurgitation noted. Right Ventricle: Normal size. PASP: 35 mmHg.
clinical_reports.conclusion: Severe Calcified Aortic Stenosis (AVA 0.8 cm², Mean Gradient 44 mmHg) on Bicuspid Aortic Valve with Preserved LV Ejection Fraction (60%).

clinical_reports.findings: Left Ventricle: Normal LV cavity dimensions. LVEF: 60%. Left Atrium: Normal (34 mm). Aortic Valve: Calcified bicuspid aortic valve with severe aortic stenosis. Peak velocity: 4.3 m/s, Mean gradient: 44 mmHg, Aortic valve area (AVA): 0.8 cm². Mitral Valve: Mild aortic regurgitation noted. Right Ventricle: Normal size. PASP: 35 mmHg.

clinical_reports.findings: Left Ventricle: LVEF: 38% (moderately reduced). Hypokinesia noted in anterior and anteroseptal wall segments. Left Atrium: Dilated (LA volume index 42 ml/m²). Mitral Valve: Secondary functional mitral regurgitation (Moderate MR). Right Ventricle: TAPSE 15 mm (borderline RV systolic function). Pericardium: Trace pericardial effusion.
clinical_reports.conclusion: Ischemic Cardiomyopathy with Moderately Reduced LVEF (38%), Anterior Wall Hypokinesia, and Moderate Functional Mitral Regurgitation.

clinical_reports.findings: Left Ventricle: Concentric LV hypertrophy present. LVEF: 62%. Regional wall motion normal. Left Atrium: Mildly dilated (LA dimension 38 mm). Valves: Aortic valve trileaflet with mild sclerosis, no stenosis. Mitral valve shows mild posterior leaflet prolapse with mild regurgitation (MR). Right Ventricle & Atrium: Normal dimensions. PASP: 28 mmHg. Pericardium: No pericardial effusion.
clinical_reports.conclusion: Concentric LV Hypertrophy with preserved LV Systolic Function (LVEF 62%), Grade I Diastolic Dysfunction, and Mild Mitral Regurgitation.

clinical_reports.findings: Left Ventricle: LVEF: 55%. Normal global systolic function. Grade II Diastolic Dysfunction (pseudonormal pattern, E/A 1.4, Dec time 150 ms, E/e' 15). Left Atrium: Dilated (LA volume index 39 ml/m²). Valves: Mild Tricuspid Regurgitation. PASP: 38 mmHg (mild pulmonary hypertension). Pericardium: Intact.
clinical_reports.conclusion: Grade II LV Diastolic Dysfunction with Left Atrial Enlargement and Mild Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension.

clinical_reports.findings: Left Ventricle: Normal LV size and wall thickness. LVEF: 65% (normal). No regional wall motion abnormality. Left Atrium: Normal size (32 mm). Valves: Normal aortic, mitral, and tricuspid valve morphology and doppler velocities. Right Ventricle: Normal RV size and function. PASP: 22 mmHg. Pericardium: No pericardial effusion.
clinical_reports.conclusion: Normal Transthoracic Echocardiogram. Preserved Biventricular Systolic Function (LVEF 65%). No Valvular Heart Disease.

clinical_reports.findings: Pediatric Echocardiogram. Normal cardiac position and segmental connections. Interatrial septum: 4.2 mm ostium secundum ASD with left-to-right shunt. No significant RV enlargement. Interventricular septum intact. LVEF: 66%. Aortic arch left-sided without coarctation.

clinical_reports.findings: Pediatric Echocardiogram. Cardiac position normal. Small muscular VSD (2.5 mm) in mid-septum with restrictive left-to-right jet (peak velocity 4.1 m/s, gradient 67 mmHg). Normal LV and RV dimensions. LVEF: 68%. Intact interatrial septum. No Patent Ductus Arteriosus (PDA).
clinical_reports.conclusion: Small Restrictive Muscular Ventricular Septal Defect (VSD) with high-velocity left-to-right shunt. Excellent biventricular function.
